reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires modifying the bootloader configuration (kernel argument fips=1). This change is permanent, can render the system unbootable if applied incorrectly, requires a reboot to take effect, and may disrupt cryptographic policies. Because it involves bootloader/FIPS changes, it is classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; bootloader modification; FIPS mode; system lockout; requires reboot; cryptographic policy change
